description: Fix flaky browser_execute_script E2E tests: (1) tab-closed error regex doesn't match 'Frame with ID 0 was removed', (2) async Promise rejection race condition causes intermittent 10s timeout instead of catching the rejection, (3) async Promise returns undefined on strict-CSP pages. After this lands, all browser_execute_script tests pass reliably on all platforms.

userStories.title: Fix tab-closed error regex in browser-tools.e2e.ts stress test
userStories.description: The test 'browser_execute_script on tab closed mid-execution returns error within 10s' fails because the error message 'Frame with ID 0 was removed' does not match the test's regex `/tab|cannot access/i`. The regex needs to also accept frame-removal messages from Chromium.
acceptanceCriteria: The regex at the assertion matches 'Frame with ID 0 was removed' in addition to existing patterns
The negative assertion (not matching 'timed out') is preserved
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/browser-tools.e2e.ts (fix regex around line 3339)

Context:
The test at line 3310 closes a tab during a slow script execution and asserts the error message:
```ts
// Error must reference tab being gone, not a generic timeout
const errorText = JSON.stringify(result.content);
expect(errorText).toMatch(/tab|cannot access/i);
expect(errorText).not.toMatch(/timed out/i);
```

Chromium returns 'Frame with ID 0 was removed.' when a tab is closed mid-execution. This message contains neither 'tab' nor 'cannot access'.

Changes:
Update line 3339 from:
```ts
expect(errorText).toMatch(/tab|cannot access/i);
```
To:
```ts
expect(errorText).toMatch(/tab|cannot access|frame.*removed/i);
```

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify the negative assertion on line 3340 (`not.toMatch(/timed out/i)`)
- Do NOT change any other test in the file

userStories.title: Fix async Promise rejection race condition in browser_execute_script
userStories.description: The test 'handles async code that rejects' in browser-tools.e2e.ts is flaky — the Promise rejection is sometimes not caught before the 10s polling timeout fires, returning 'Async code did not resolve within 10000ms' instead of the rejection error 'async-fail'. The same root cause affects 'handles async code (Promises)' on macOS and 'async code (Promise) resolves correctly on strict-CSP page' in strict-csp.e2e.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: browser_execute_script correctly returns Promise rejection errors instead of timing out
browser_execute_script correctly returns async resolved values on strict-CSP pages
The exec file wrapper correctly sets __openTabs[resultKey] for both resolved and rejected Promises
All three affected tests pass reliably: browser-tools.e2e.ts:604, browser-tools.e2e.ts:582, strict-csp.e2e.ts:558
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/extension-commands.ts (investigate polling logic)
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/ (investigate exec file generation)
- e2e/browser-tools.e2e.ts (the flaky tests at lines 582, 604)
- e2e/strict-csp.e2e.ts (the failing test at line 558)

Context:
The `browser_execute_script` command works in two phases:
1. Generates an exec JS file (wrapper around user code) and writes it to the adapters/ directory
2. Injects the file into the tab's MAIN world via `chrome.scripting.executeScript({ files: [...] })`
3. Polls for the result via repeated `chrome.scripting.executeScript({ func: ... })` calls

The wrapper sets `__openTabs[resultKey]` when the user code completes:
- For sync code: sets immediately
- For async code (Promises): sets `__openTabs[asyncKey] = true` and resolves/rejects later

The polling logic (extension-commands.ts around line 414-483) checks:
```ts
const result = ot[rKey] as { value?: unknown; error?: string } | undefined;
const isAsync = ot[aKey] === true;

// Result available (sync or async resolved)
if (result && ('value' in result || 'error' in result)) { ... }

// Async code hasn't resolved yet
if (isAsync) return { pending: true };
```

The race condition: the `isAsync` flag may be set AFTER the polling check reads it as undefined, causing the poller to return `{ pending: false, result: { error: 'No result captured' } }` or miss the first few polls. On CSP pages, file injection may be slightly slower, widening the race window.

Investigation steps:
1. Read the exec file generation code in platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/ to understand the wrapper that sets resultKey/asyncKey/startedKey
2. Check if the wrapper correctly handles Promise.reject() — the rejection might not set the error on resultKey
3. Check timing: the `startedKey` is checked (line 452) but the `asyncKey` might not be set atomically with `startedKey`
4. The EXEC_POLL_INTERVAL_MS constant controls polling frequency — check if it's too slow

Potential fixes:
- Ensure the exec wrapper sets asyncKey BEFORE starting the async operation
- Ensure Promise.catch() correctly writes to resultKey with error
- Consider reducing EXEC_POLL_INTERVAL_MS for faster detection

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the EXEC_MAX_ASYNC_WAIT_MS (10s) — that's the intended maximum wait
- Do NOT change the SCRIPT_TIMEOUT_MS outer timeout
- The fix must work for both regular pages and strict-CSP pages
- The exec file approach (bypassing CSP via file injection) is the correct architecture — do NOT switch to inline func injection
